food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — burger, large fries, and diet carbonated beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger entry (FOODON_00002383). No specific entry exists for a creamy mushroom beef burger combo meal. The hamburger class is used as the closest defensible anchor for the burger component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKV covers hamburgers and similar products. No specific code exists for a mushroom-topped burger combo meal. This is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for a creamy mushroom burger combo meal. SNOMED CT does not provide granular fast-food product codes at this level.

preparation_method: assembled burger with creamy mushroom sauce

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (burger + large fries + Coke Zero)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger (approximately 220 g), one large portion of McDonald's fries (approximately 154 g), and one Coke Zero (approximately 306 g / 300 ml), totalling approximately 680 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger, Large Fries, Coke Zero (estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and branded entries)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Branded Foods) with McDonald's Singapore nutritional panel reference
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore does not publish a single composite per-100g figure for this upsized meal. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA Branded Foods entry for McDonald's Quarter Pounder with Cheese as a proxy for a beef burger with sauce (FDC ID 2103726, adapted for mushroom sauce variant); (2) USDA Branded Foods entry for McDonald's French Fries Large (FDC ID 2103729); (3) Coke Zero contributing negligible macros. Per-100g values are computed from the weighted composite of burger (~220g) + large fries (~154g) + Coke Zero (~306g, ~0 kcal) across the 680g total serving. This is an estimated composite. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white wheat bread and fast-food burger bun analogues; French fries GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Upsized Meal. GI estimated as a weighted composite: burger bun (white wheat bread analogue, GI ~71, University of Sydney GI Database), beef patty and mushroom sauce (negligible glycemic contribution, GI ~0), large French fries (GI ~63 per University of Sydney GI Database for fast-food French fries), and Coke Zero (GI ~0, no glycemic carbohydrates). Weighted composite GI across glycemic carbohydrate contributors yields approximately 63 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (63 × 29.0) / 100 ≈ 18.3. Available carbohydrate per serving estimated at 29.0 g (burger bun ~22 g + fries ~7 g net glycemic carbs after fibre adjustment).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 1,972 mg sodium per upsized meal serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 85–99% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2,000 mg, which can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI ~63) combined with high refined carbohydrate load from burger bun and large fries, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 18.3 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only; the combination of refined carbohydrates from the bun and large fries produces a significant postprandial glucose excursion, and the high saturated fat content may further impair insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~16.3 g per serving), trans fat (~1.4 g per serving), and sodium (~1,972 mg per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this meal due to its high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content, all of which are independently associated with increased LDL cholesterol, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McDonald's Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Upsized Meal is a fast-food combo meal served at McDonald's Singapore, consisting of the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger paired with a large portion of McDonald's classic French fries and a Coke Zero. The burger features a seasoned beef patty topped with a rich, savoury creamy mushroom sauce, crisp lettuce, and served in a soft wheat bun. The large fries are deep-fried, lightly salted, and golden-crisp. Coke Zero contributes no calories or macronutrients. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, the upsized meal is energy-dense, providing approximately 1,006 kcal per serving, with significant contributions from fat (approximately 47 g), carbohydrates (approximately 112 g), and protein (approximately 39 g). Sodium content is high at approximately 1,972 mg per serving. The meal is ultra-processed and high in saturated fat and refined carbohydrates, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index of the meal is estimated at approximately 63 (medium), driven primarily by the refined wheat bun and large fries. Clinically, this meal should be consumed only occasionally and in the context of an otherwise balanced dietary pattern. It is a popular fast-food indulgence in Singapore and is widely available through field survey delivery from McDonald's outlets island-wide.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore does not publish a single composite per-100g nutritional panel for this specific upsized meal combination. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central branded and SR Legacy entries for McDonald's burger and large fries components, weighted by serving mass. The Coke Zero component contributes negligible macros. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using fast-food French fries and white wheat bread as component proxies. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific composite meal.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this composite estimate. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Per-100g values are derived from a weighted composite of burger (~220g) + large fries (~154g) + Coke Zero (~306g) across a 680g total serving. 5. The food_group from the input JSON was listed as 'Vegetables', which is incorrect for this combo meal; corrected to 'Mixed'. 6. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific McDonald's meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Coke Zero contributes approximately 0 kcal, 0g protein, 0g fat, and 0g carbohydrate to the composite; its inclusion dilutes per-100g macros relative to the burger and fries components alone. 8. Sodium per serving (~1,972 mg) is estimated from USDA branded data for McDonald's burger and large fries components. 9. Aliases consistency check (iteration 3): all strings present in aliases_by_language have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. The Hindi entry 'क्रीमी मशरूम डीलक्स मील' (previously rendered inconsistently as 'क्रीमी मशरूम डीलux मील' in the previous draft's aliases_by_language) has been corrected to use full Devanagari script throughout both aliases_by_language and the flat aliases list.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced with all sections populated.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type values from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Re-verified aliases consistency.

Iteration 3: Identified root cause of Q8 failure — Hindi alias in aliases_by_language contained mixed Devanagari/Latin script ('क्रीमी मशरूम डीलux मील'). Corrected to full Devanagari ('क्रीमी मशरूम डीलक्स मील') in both aliases_by_language.hindi and flat aliases list. Performed exhaustive string-by-string cross-check of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases array to confirm perfect consistency.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
